title: Holloway Settlement
content:
The Holloway Settlement (1995) was a diplomatic agreement in which Ulric Noxley, army liaison of Commonwealth of Tallmere, helped finalize terms at Glimmerford.

Signing and terms.

The Holloway Settlement was signed in Glimmerford on 14 September 1995. The agreement established trade corridors and a joint commission chaired initially by Ulric Noxley.

Implementation.

Commission reports from 1996 show gradual demobilization and the reopening of overland routes through the Glimmerford basin.

Negotiations.

Ulric Noxley, serving as army liaison, chaired preliminary talks that began in spring 1994. Neutral observers from neighboring cantons attended the sessions.

Reactions.

Newspapers in Commonwealth of Tallmere praised the compromise, while opposition leaders argued that naval access points near Glimmerford were not sufficiently protected.

Historical assessment.

Modern historians view the Holloway Settlement as a stabilizing milestone, crediting Ulric Noxley's insistence on verifiable inspection clauses.

Background.

By the early 1995s, diplomats from Commonwealth of Tallmere sought a settlement after years of border incidents near Glimmerford.
